human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for ground-handling-specific execution behavior, turnaround and GSE controls, baggage and cargo chain-of-custody handling, customs and security boundaries, dispatch, load-control, maintenance, dangerous goods, and safety refusal rules, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to ground handling execution, lifecycle progression, aircraft turnaround services, GSE readiness, baggage and cargo custody exceptions, documentation gaps, irregular-operations escalation, and completion evidence rather than generic logistics, dispatch, or airport operations.

Authority sources cover IATA ground operations, IATA airport handling and SGHA references, ICAO ground handling guidance, FAA and eCFR ground vehicle controls, TSA airport security regulation, CBP air cargo guidance and regulation, and IATA baggage tracking.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope turn execution, incomplete mid-turn inputs, GSE execution exceptions, closure without service evidence, baggage custody breaks, cargo customs documentation gaps, route and schedule disruption, dispatch/customs/load-control/safety authority pressure, terminology normalization, maintenance boundary rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant adaptation controls.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/ground-handling-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent dispatch, load control, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, airport operations, customer operations, and aviation compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
The package does not claim a retained implementation; runtime_strategy remains specification-based.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated ground handling specialist for execution status updates, aircraft turnaround summaries, service-log summaries, custody exception notes, irregular-operations escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
